Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 154. Soldiers Idling Time Away in Calah (CT 53 829)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
oo beginning broken away

(Beginning destroyed)

o 1'1'

šu-[x x x x]

o 2'2'

LÚv.[x x x x]

(2) The [troops] who a[rrived ...] and have been resid[ent] in [...] are loitering in the center of Calah with their riding horses like [...] common criminals and drunkards.

o 3'3'

⸢ša⸣ [x x x x]

o 4'4'

im-[qu-tu-u-ni]

o 5'5'

ina ⸢URU⸣.[x x x]

o 6'6'

kam-[mu-su-ni]

o 7'7'

a-ki ⸢ša⸣ [x x x]-di

o 8'8'

LÚv.⸢LUL⸣-[MEŠ]

Bottom
b.e. 9'b.e. 9'

LÚv.šá-ki-ru-tú

Reverse
r 1r 1

ANŠE.pet-ḫal-lu

r 22

i-si-šu-nu

r 33

ina qab-si URU.kal-ḫi

r 44

i-du-u-lu

r 55

mi-nu ša be-lí

(r 5) What does my lord say? [Let my lord quickly send an answ]er to my letter!

r 66

i-qa-bu-u-ni

r 77

[gab-ru]-ú ša e-gir-te

r 88

[be-lí ár-ḫiš liš-pu-ra]

rest broken away

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P314238/.
